I remember this spot.
One day, my mom and I sat on this bench for hours.
She and Dad had been fighting for days. I think she just needed to get away.
She always said she loved him, though.
That doesn’t sound like love.
Maybe not. But then again… I don’t think I really know what love is.
I used to walk this trail with my mom and my brother. Back then, it felt… different.
Why are you walking alone?
I haven’t seen them in a while.
Why is that?
I moved away. I thought it was the right thing to do.
I thought I would be better for it.
I can see the bottom now. It doesn’t feel as far away.
It’s almost like I can breathe again, but it’s still… heavy.
The light’s fading, but I think it’s different this time.
Maybe it’s not as bad as before. I just need to keep going.
Going down feels… harder than going up.
It’s steeper than I thought.
Each step feels like I’m falling, even though I’m still in control.
I guess it’s not always about climbing up. Sometimes, it’s just about staying steady on the way down.
I used to come here all the time.
When I was younger, this trail felt like home.
From the top, you can see everything—even my house.
It’s been a while, though. I’m so tired lately.
I don’t even go outside much anymore.
Who are you talking to?
Why not?
I don’t know… I just don’t have time.
It’s strange, though—I barely do anything, really.
And yet… I never seem to have the time for anything.
It’s strange how the world keeps moving, even when I don’t. Out here… it feels different.
Are you okay?
Are YOU okay?
Yes. (Pause) …No.
No. I don’t think I am. But it’s hard to explain.
I don’t know. Maybe? Maybe not.
It’s funny—being out here makes the world seem brighter.
Not literally… but in a way that feels real. It’s comforting, even if it’s just in my head.
I don’t know why I don’t come out here more.
